definitions: To place something on a surface.
To write something down; to record.
To suppress a rebellion or protest by force.
To humanely kill an animal, especially one that is old, sick, or injured.
To pay a deposit for something.
examples: Please put down the book on the table.
I need to put down these ideas before I forget them.
Don't let anyone put you down.
The government quickly put down the uprising.
We had to put down our old dog last year.
She put down a deposit on the new car.
pastTenses: put down (past simple), put down (present perfect)
